business.headline: Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningInfosys fell harder than every other major IT stock after Accenture's warning. But if the entire sector was hit by the same trigger, why did Infosys see the sharpest fall?

Reliance AGM 2026 Live: Investors await details on Jio IPORIL AGM 2026 Live Updates: Mukesh Ambani set to address shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th annual general meeting; Jio IPO, AI strategy and retail business in focus.

Sensex down 700 points: Why is stock market falling today after a 5-day rally?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 635.85 points, or 0.84%, at 76,774.13, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 170.10 points, or 0.70%, to 23,997.90 as of 10:13 am. The decline snapped a strong rally that had seen the Sensex gain nearly 4.8% and the Nifty rise over 4.3% in the previous five sessions.

Reliance AGM: Will Mukesh Ambani finally reveal the Jio IPO date today?Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to address shareholders at the company's 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM) today, and the biggest question on Dalal Street is simple: Will he finally announce a timeline for the Jio IPO?

Accenture warning sends Infosys, TCS and HCLTech tumbling. Here's whyMajor Indian IT stocks fell sharply after Accenture lowered the upper end of its annual revenue growth forecast. The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Accenture warning triggers IT crash on Dalal StreetInfosys, TCS and other technology stocks tumbled after the global IT giant flagged slower growth, dragging the broader market lower despite falling oil prices.

India's Swiss bank funds fall 8%, while direct deposits jump 50%Swiss National Bank data showed total funds linked to Indian clients fell in 2025 even as direct customer deposits rose sharply. The figures underline that Indian-linked assets in Switzerland are not the same as black money estimates and reflect a more transparent banking regime.

Jio IPO, AI, green energy: 5 big questions Mukesh Ambani may answer at RIL AGMMukesh Ambani is set to face shareholder questions on Jio's IPO timeline, AI plans and green energy projects at Reliance's 49th AGM. With key deadlines nearing, investors are seeking proof that earlier commitments are moving into execution.

Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

NSE IPO filing could unlock Rs 25,000 crore for SBI, Temasek and top investorsNSE has filed draft papers for its long-delayed initial public offering after nearly a decade. The move could unlock major gains for existing investors and value the exchange at about $57 billion.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have sought a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission considers revisions to salaries and pensions. The final multiplier will determine how much basic pay and pensions rise for millions of central government employees and retirees.

Sensex closes 254 points higher, Nifty near 24,200; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 254.36 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,409.98, while the NSE Nifty50 gained around 82 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 24,168.

Crude oil is no longer the villain. A new threat could raise your household billEconomists say a weak monsoon has overtaken crude oil as India's main inflation risk. Poor rainfall could raise food prices, squeeze household budgets and complicate RBI rate cuts.

EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

Why the Rs 30,000-crore NSE IPO is unlike any other IPOFor nearly 10 years, the NSE IPO was the market's longest-running "coming soon" story. Now, India's biggest exchange is finally ready to test its value in public.

EPFO 3.0 may let you withdraw PF through UPI, ATM soon. Here's what could changeEPFO is preparing to launch its 3.0 platform with new digital withdrawal options for subscribers. The upgrade could speed up claims and simplify access to provident fund savings.

Why Trump said 'whatever' after new US Fed chief did not cut ratesUS President Donald Trump got a new Federal Reserve chief, but not the lower-rate signal he had been hoping for. What changed was his reaction.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech down: Why are IT stocks falling after the recent rally?IT stocks fell at the opening bell. Among the biggest losers were Infosys, which fell 2.31%, Tech Mahindra was down 1.42%, HCLTech slid 1.17%, TCS lost 1.20%, while Persistent Systems and Wipro also lost 1.68% and 0.68% respectively in early trade.

entertainment.headline: Cocktail 2 X review: Cheers for Kriti, side-eye for Rashmika's accent Cocktail 2 opened in theatres on June 19 and received mixed reactions on X. Much of the online chatter praised Kriti Sanon, while the screenplay and Rashmika Mandanna's accent faced criticism.

Ranveer Singh's drastic 18-month no-interview pledge amid Don 3 row Ranveer Singh has reportedly decided not to speak on the Don 3 controversy or give interviews for 18 months. The silence comes as his fallout with Farhan Akhtar has widened into a legal dispute ahead of Pralay.

Shraddha Kapoor's Eetha teaser leaked online, stunned fans call it promising as hell The leaked teaser of Eetha shows actor Shraddha Kapoor as tamasha artiste Vithabai Bhau Mang Narayangaonkar during a stage performance around childbirth. The glimpse has gone viral, drawing strong fan reactions and raising buzz around the film.

Karan Kundrra defends seeking parents' nod before Desi Bling proposal to Tejasswi Karan Kundrra has responded to criticism over consulting his parents before proposing to Tejasswi Prakash. His remarks have sparked fresh conversation about family and relationships.

SRK waived Rs 42 lakh for Deool Band 2; Marathi film went on to earn Rs 80 crore Filmmaker Pravin Tarde said Shah Rukh Khan asked Red Chillies Entertainment to waive pending DCP dues for Deool Band 2 before its release. The gesture helped the Marathi film reach cinemas despite a budget crunch and is now being remembered after its box-office success.

Angelina Jolie lost fighting spirit after Brad Pitt divorce, children healed her Angelina Jolie said a difficult period after her divorce from Brad Pitt left her feeling taken down for a time. She said her children have helped revive her confidence as she discusses a role in Couture that echoes parts of her life.

Toy Story 5 review: A relevant and thoughtful sequel for the iPad generationRelease Date:Fri Jun 19, 2026 Toy Story 5 review: Pixar explores children's growing attachment to technology as Woody, Buzz and Jessie confront a new threat to playtime. Read our review.

Ranbir as Rama, Alia as 'Alpha' Sita: Did star couple just amp the power quotient? On-screen names of stars add to image. Think Big B's Vijay or SRK's Rahul. Will playing Rama and Sita boost Ranbir Kapoor and Alia Bhatt's prospects as a Bollywood duo?

Lesbian romance or love triangle? Kriti Sanon reacts to Cocktail 2 rumours Actor Kriti Sanon has addressed speculation surrounding Cocktail 2's central relationship, setting the record straight at a promotional event. Her clarification comes days before the film's theatrical release.

YRF plays its Hrithik Roshan card in Alpha trailer. Desperate move or masterstroke? The Alpha trailer revealed Hrithik Roshan's cameo alongside Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Spy Universe debut. The move has shifted attention from scepticism around the film to speculation over what YRF may still be holding back.

Director Shankar's son Arjith to make acting debut with Krithi Shetty in Lover Boy Director Shankar's son Arjith is set to enter films with Lover Boy, starring Krithi Shetty. The project has officially begun, but several key details remain under wraps.

Akshay Kumar seeks blessings at Vaishno Devi ahead of Welcome to the Jungle Actor Akshay Kumar offered prayers at the Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine under heavy security. He will be seen next in Welcome to the Jungle, releasing in theatres end of June.

Sara Ali Khan meets Henry Cavill, brings Enola Holmes vibe in vintage look. Pics Sara Ali Khan shared photographs with Henry Cavill from a Longines campaign styled in a vintage mood. The pairing has drawn attention as a new East-meets-West crossover with an Enola Holmes-like feel.

Row over director's inappropriate behaviour with actors Keerthy Suresh, Krithi Shetty A video from director Anil Ravipudi's Hyderabad film launch shows veteran filmmaker K Raghavendra Rao pulling actors Keerthy Suresh and Krithi Shetty closer by holding their arms. The clip triggered criticism online, with users slamming him for his inappropriate behaviour.

Cocktail 2 box office prediction: Pre-sales lower than Tere Ishk Mein, Thamma Shahid Kapoor's Cocktail 2 has earned over Rs 3 crore ahead of its June 19 release. Box office trade estimates suggest a moderate start, with advance bookings trailing Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna's Tere Ishk Mein and Thamma respectively.

Ram Charan's Peddi gets 6 minutes of new footage: What's been added and edited Nearly two weeks after its release, Peddi's theatrical version is updated with close to six minutes of additional footage. Fans say the new version restores some important scenes while removing a few controversial moments.

Dhanush's D55 with Rajkumar Periasamy titled Om, teaser hints at social unrest Actor Dhanush and director Rajkumar Periasamy's film has been titled Om. The makers announced the title along with a gritty first-look teaser on Thursday.

I, Nobody trailer: Prithviraj Sukumaran plays a man caught in a web of crime The official trailer of I, Nobody introduces Prithviraj Sukumaran as Rajeevan, a man trapped in crime and personal turmoil. The promo points to a character-led thriller where family ties and psychological conflict drive the suspense.

Tanya Mittal defends Pranit More amid social media backlash: Feel bad for him Bigg Boss 19 contestant Tanya Mittal said she feels bad for comedian Pranit More even as she called his conduct wrong. Her remarks come as the row over the viral crowd-work clip has led to FIRs, apologies and a consent debate.

general news.headline: Setback for Telegram, court says government's temporary ban isn't disproportionateThe government had imposed the temporary restriction citing apprehensions that Telegram was being used by organised cheating networks allegedly involved in the NEET-UG controversy.

Rajya Sabha results in 3 states expose Congress's puzzling contrastsDespite having the numbers, Congress suffered setbacks in Rajya Sabha contests in both Jharkhand and Madhya Pradesh. Yet, on the same day, it secured 16 extra votes to win comfortably in Karnataka's Legislative Council polls, highlighting stark contrasts in the party's political and alliance management.

The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Monsoon stuck: How five weather systems teamed up to stop India's rainsIndia's monsoon advance has stalled after an early onset as multiple weather systems disrupted its flow. The pause has widened rainfall deficits, though forecasters expect conditions to improve in late June.

Israel carries out strikes in Lebanon days after US-Iran peace agreement, 15 killedThe Israeli military said the strikes targeted what it described as Hezbollah militants and infrastructure in southern Lebanon. It added that the attacks were launched in response to what it called repeated violations of the ceasefire by the Iran-backed group.

Frankenstein state: India's takedown of Pak at UN on terrorismIndia told the UN Human Rights Council that Pakistan had harboured and nurtured terrorism as state policy. It said Islamabad's claim of being a victim exposed a paradox that only Pakistan could sustain.

Why Dyer was felicitated at Golden Temple days after Jallianwala Bagh massacreDays after the Jallianwala Bagh massacre in 1919, Golden Temple managers invited Brigadier General Reginald Dyer and honoured him with a siropa and kirpan. The priests even urged the 'Butcher of Amritsar' to become a Sikh and agreed to waive the requirement of unshorn hair when he said he could not follow it as a serving British officer.

Cocktail 2 Movie Release and Review Live: Pulkit Samrat's cameo steals the showCocktail 2 Movie Release and Review Live Updates: Get the latest first-day-first-show public reviews, critical ratings, theatre occupancy, and Day 1 box office collections for Homi Adajania's new film.

Does China have a caste system or is it a figment of imagination of Indians?China, the world's manufacturing powerhouse and a socialist state governed by a communist party, is being accused online of having a birth-based caste system that shapes access to education, healthcare, and opportunity. Does China really have a caste system? Here's the reality.

Sreesanth dares Harbhajan to ring fight: Have you got the guts?Sreesanth has openly challenged Harbhajan Singh to enter the ring after taking offence at an advertisement he says referenced the 2008 slapgate episode. The outburst drags one of Indian cricket's ugliest off-field rows back into view and raises fresh questions about profiting from old humiliation.

IIM Ahmedabad professor uses Dhurandhar scene to teach statistics in class. WatchAn IIM Ahmedabad professor used a scene from Dhurandhar to explain statistical concepts such as cluster sampling and stratification. The video went viral as viewers praised the inventive way complex concepts were broken down.

Catch-22 for Congress as Tamil Nadu's Vijay takes on Karnataka over MekedatuThe Tamil Nadu Assembly unanimously opposed Karnataka's Mekedatu dam project and urged the Centre to withhold approval. The move sharpens the Cauvery dispute and leaves Congress divided across the two states.

What is black rain? Oily drizzle seen over Moscow after Ukraine's attackA Ukrainian drone strike on Moscow's Kapotnya refinery was followed by reports of dark, oily drizzle across nearby districts. The fallout drew attention to black rain, a form of precipitation contaminated by smoke and soot.
Kancheepuram leads India's infidelity rankings againKancheepuram has again recorded the highest Ashley Madison sign-ups in India for May 2026.

Deepinder Goyal's Temple biomarker claim faces scientific questionsDeepinder Goyal said Temple has identified an 'Entropy' biomarker that tracks the body's real-time energy use. Doctors said the claim remains unproven without methodology, validation studies and peer-reviewed evidence.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have sought a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission considers revisions to salaries and pensions. The final multiplier will determine how much basic pay and pensions rise for millions of central government employees and retirees.

New season, new rival, own style: Neeraj Chopra eyes grand return in DohaNeeraj Chopra returns to action in Doha for the first time since an injury-marred eighth at last year's World Championships. The two-time Olympic medallist is fit again and chasing his own style, with Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage arriving as the season's biggest threat.

Running on ethanol and electricity: Why flex-fuel hybrids matterAs India accelerates ethanol adoption and cleaner mobility solutions, flex-fuel hybrid vehicles are emerging as a promising alternative, combining ethanol-powered engines with hybrid technology to reduce emissions and fuel imports.

Telegram edit feature led to its ban in India, NTA says students losing money to itTelegram is banned in India ahead of the NEET re-test on June 21. A major reason for this ban, according to the National Testing Agency (NTA) is the editing feature on the app. Here is how the edit feature works, and why it is now at the centre of controversy.

UAE sets social media age limit at 15, a first among Arab nationsThe new rules impose a near-total social media ban on children under 15, while 15- and 16-year-olds will face stricter safeguards, including parental oversight, limits on interactions with strangers, and tools to manage screen time and online content.

sports.headline: First Singh to play in FIFA World Cup: NZ's Sarpreet wants more Indians there FIFA World Cup 2026: Sarpreet Singh said his World Cup appearance for New Zealand carried deep meaning for his family and community. His milestone as the first Sikh on this stage has widened the conversation on South Asian representation in football.

Get MS Dhoni as mentor: Sreesanth says India do not need coach Gautam Gambhir Former India fast bowler S Sreesanth has criticised head coach Gautam Gambhir's role in the national team setup, saying India would be better off with a mentor like MS Dhoni rather than a traditional coach.

The making of Shivang Kumar: SRH backing, Cummins' trust and an India dream Shivang Kumar did not even attend two Sunrisers Hyderabad trials. Yet, by the end of his debut IPL season, the left-arm wrist-spinner had become a trusted member of Pat Cummins' XI. In an exclusive interaction with IndiaToday.in, Shivang said he is in no rush to realise his India dream, choosing instead to focus on learning, improving, and making the most of the opportunities coming his way.

New season, new rival, own style: Neeraj Chopra eyes grand return in Doha Neeraj Chopra returns to action in Doha for the first time since an injury-marred eighth at last year's World Championships. The two-time Olympic medallist is fit again and chasing his own style, with Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage arriving as the season's biggest threat.

Hydration or ad breaks? Who are the real winners of FIFA World Cup's new rule FIFA's mandatory World Cup hydration breaks have split opinion among players, coaches and supporters, with some welcoming the tactical opportunities while others argue they disrupt the flow of football.

Manika Batra deeply disappointed by Asian Games snub, alleges lack of transparency Asian Games 2026: Manika Batra has sought Prime Minister Narendra Modi's intervention after being left out of India's Asian Games table tennis squad. Her appeal has sharpened questions over transparency and the uneven application of TTFI's selection rules.

Forget the Rs 799 app: Here's how to watch FIFA World Cup in HD for just Rs 24 FIFA World Cup 2026: A clever broadcast loophole lets football fans bypass expensive streaming packages and buggy apps, delivering the world's biggest tournament in flawless high-definition for less than the price of a daily cup of tea.

Harshit Rana added to India squad for third ODI against Afghanistan Harshit Rana has been added to India's squad for the third and final ODI against Afghanistan after completing his rehabilitation at the BCCI Centre of Excellence (CoE). The fast bowler has joined the team in Chennai ahead of the series finale.

Sreesanth dares Harbhajan to ring fight: Have you got the guts? Sreesanth has openly challenged Harbhajan Singh to enter the ring after taking offence at an advertisement he says referenced the 2008 slapgate episode. The outburst drags one of Indian cricket's ugliest off-field rows back into view and raises fresh questions about profiting from old humiliation.

Inspired by Virat Kohli, India's Manpreet Singh redefines longevity in hockey Modelling himself on Virat Kohli, Manpreet Singh continues to defy age in his bid to keep his LA Olympics dream alive. India's most-capped player, however, insists that his fitness will ultimately determine how long the journey continues.

Mexico edge South Korea 1-0 to become first side to qualify for last 32 stage FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico became the first team to qualify for the Round of 32 after securing a hard-fought 1-0 win over South Korea in their Group A clash at Akron Stadium in Guadalajara on Friday.

Messi vs Ronaldo rivalry brings out the worst in fans and pundits alikeFIFA World Cup 2026: Thierry Henry's criticism of Cristiano Ronaldo reignited football's biggest divide, exposing how the Messi-Ronaldo rivalry continues to blur the line between fair analysis and personal judgement.

Scotland fans cause beer crisis in Boston during historic World Cup returnFIFA World Cup 2026: Scotland's World Cup return has sent Boston bars rushing for emergency beer deliveries. The surge has delighted local businesses and introduced the city to the Tartan Army's travelling fervour.

Kone injury takes shine off Canada's historic 6-goal World Cup rout vs QatarCanada dismantled Qatar as Jonathan David's hat-trick drove a ruthless win in Vancouver. Yet Ismael Kone's injury left Canada sweating over a key midfielder despite moving top of Group B.

Canada's Ismael Kone suffers gruesome injury, players left distraughtFIFA World Cup 2026: Canada midfielder Ismael Kone suffered a serious-looking leg injury after Assim Madibo's reckless challenge in the second half. The straight red card and the shocked reaction around the ground underlined the severity of the incident.

Taylor, Alleyne star as West Indies survive scare from spirited ScotlandStafanie Taylor's late assault and Aaliyah Alleyne's 19th-over burst carried West Indies past Scotland at Headingley. Scotland pushed a higher-ranked side deep, but missed chances and a stalled finish proved costly.

Shreyanka ruled out of T20 World Cup, India name leg-spinner as replacementShreyanka Patil has been ruled out of the Women's T20 World Cup after injuring her ankle against the Netherlands. India have brought in uncapped leg-spinner Prema Rawat as the replacement for the bowling all-rounder.

Super-sub Manzambi fires Switzerland to 4-1 win vs Bosnia and HerzegovinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Johan Manzambi came off the bench to score twice as Switzerland beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1 in Los Angeles. The win put Switzerland in charge of Group B and left Bosnia under pressure ahead of Qatar.

Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi gets Canada entry despite spot-fixing probeIvory Coast forward Elye Wahi has been cleared to travel to Canada after his authorisation came through. The late approval arrived amid a French betting investigation, though he has not been named a suspect.

MEX 1-0 KOR Highlights: Mexcio qualifies for Round of 32 after edging past South KoreaMexico (MEX) vs South Korea (KOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico have completed their qualification for the round of 32 after edging past South Korea after taking the lead from a clumsy keeping effor by Korean keeper allowing Luis Romo to put it in the back of the net.

ENG vs NZ: Glenn Phillips, Matt Henry turn the screws on England at the OvalENG vs NZ, 2nd Test Day 2: Glenn Phillips scored his maiden Test century and Matt Henry struck twice as New Zealand moved ahead on day two at The Oval. England closed on 222 for six, still 169 behind and under pressure in the second Test.

Thomas Tuchel crashout forces FIFA to change World Cup anthem protocolThomas Tuchel's complaint about blocked views during the national anthem prompted FIFA to reposition photographers at World Cup 2026 venues. The change followed England's win over Croatia in Dallas and addressed a pre-match issue caused by the stadium layout.

South Africa's Cristiano Ronaldo tribute goes viral after Mokoena's late penaltyFIFA World Cup 2026: South Africa's Teboho Mokoena marked his late equaliser against Czechia with Cristiano Ronaldo's trademark celebrations. The moment highlighted Ronaldo's enduring pull even as Portugal opened their World Cup with a frustrating draw.

Czechia let victory slip as South Africa strike late to earn a draw in AtlantaFIFA World Cup 2026: Teboho Mokoena's late penalty rescued a 1-1 draw for South Africa against Czechia after Michal Sadilek's early strike, keeping both teams alive but under pressure in Group A.

Messi's family slam fake reports on father's health, issue positive updateLionel Messi's family said his father Jorge, is recovering under medical supervision. The statement also condemned rumours and urged privacy after intense speculation.

SUI 4-1 BIH Highlights: Manzambi at the double as Swiss thrash BosniaSwitzerland (SUI) vs Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: Manzambi's brace gives Switzerland all three points against 10-man Bosnia and Herzegovina to get their first win of the campaign. Ruben Vargas and Granit Xhaka rounded off a sensational second half performance that blew away Bosnia.

Neymar not travelling with Brazil for Haiti clash as injury rehab continuesFIFA World Cup: Brazil will be without Neymar for their second World Cup group match against Haiti as the forward continues his recovery from a calf injury, remaining in New Jersey to complete the final stage of rehabilitation.

Dropped by England after nightclub brawl, Ben Stokes returns to cricketBen Stokes has been named in Durham's squad days after England dropped him over a nightclub altercation. The county return comes as ECB investigations continue into the curfew breach and its impact on his Test future.

Pakistan players set for pay hike despite winning just two Tests last yearThe PCB has finalised new format-based central contracts with higher payments for Track A Test players. The plan also adds event-win bonuses and faces criticism over possible secrecy around categories.

Shades for Archer, history for New Zealand: Glenn Phillips completes century trebleGlenn Phillips scored his maiden Test hundred at The Oval after standing up to Jofra Archer's hostile spell. The innings took New Zealand to 391 and made him only the third Kiwi batter with international centuries in all three formats.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
